channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: Auto China 2026: BYD Fangchengbao FORMULA reveals first performance coupe
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: BYD’s premium performance sub-brand Fangchengbao today announced that its all-new FORMULA series will make its global debut on April 24 during the media day of the Beijing Auto Show, with a simultaneous live broadcast across online platforms. As the first sedan under the Fangchengbao brand, the FORMULA is positioned as a mid-to-large-sized sporty coupe. It […]
item.content:encoded: BYD’s premium performance sub-brand Fangchengbao today announced that its all-new FORMULA series will make its global debut on April 24 during the media day of the Beijing Auto Show, with a simultaneous live broadcast across online platforms. As the first sedan under the Fangchengbao brand, the FORMULA is positioned as a mid-to-large-sized sporty coupe. It is positioned to compete directly with international high-end models such as the Tesla Model S Plaid and the Porsche Taycan. The launch marks BYD’s official entry into the high-performance electric coupe segment. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: iQIYI’s AI Artist Library faces growing storm as actors deny authorization
item.dc:creator: Jessie Wu
item.description: On Monday, Chinese streaming platform iQIYI unveiled its AI Artist Library initiative with considerable fanfare, claiming that more than 100 artists had already been onboarded. The company said the program will use authorized multimodal data to build digital avatars of performers and position the library as a scalable, compliant solution for AI-assisted film and TV […]
item.content:encoded:
On Monday, Chinese streaming platform iQIYI unveiled its AI Artist Library initiative with considerable fanfare, claiming that more than 100 artists had already been onboarded.

The company said the program will use authorized multimodal data to build digital avatars of performers and position the library as a scalable, compliant solution for AI-assisted film and TV production.

The rollout, however, quickly ran into trouble.

Shortly after the announcement, Chinese actor Zhang Ruoyun’s studio issued a statement denying any involvement, saying it had never signed any AI-related authorization and that legal action was underway.

Similar denials then came from parties associated with well-known Chinese actors Wang Churan, Li Yitong, and Yu Hewei, which triggered a wave of online scrutiny and cast doubt on the accuracy of iQIYI’s claims.

Facing mounting backlash, iQIYI moved to clarify its positioning. The company framed the artist library not as a finalized roster of contracted AI performers, but as a matchmaking infrastructure for AIGC creators.

In this model, inclusion signals a potential willingness to explore AI-driven projects, while any actual participation—along with format, scope, and compensation—would still require case-by-case negotiation, similar to traditional production workflows.

That distinction, however, has done little to fully address concerns. Critics argue that marketing language such as “signed” and “onboarded” blurred the line between exploratory interest and formal authorization, creating room for misinterpretation.

Legal observers have also flagged structural risks in the model: even with consent, the reuse of an actor’s likeness and performance data in AI systems raises complex questions around ownership, control, and long-term rights management.

Beyond the immediate PR fallout, the episode shows a broader tension facing the entertainment industry. As generative AI tools move from experimentation to production, platforms such as iQIYI are racing to build the infrastructure for digital performers.

But without clear standards around consent, compensation, and governance, such efforts risk colliding with the very talent ecosystem they depend on.

In that sense, the controversy is less an isolated misstep than an early signal: scaling AI in creative industries will require not just technical capability, but also new contractual frameworks and a much tighter alignment between platform narratives and artist expectations.

item.title: Tencent’s QClaw opens international beta
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: QClaw, a consumer AI agent developed by the Tencent PC Manager team, has opened its international beta. The product is designed to let non-technical users deploy and interact with AI agents through messaging apps such as WhatsApp and Telegram without command-line setup or API configuration. The company said the international version was built in five […]
item.content:encoded:
QClaw, a consumer AI agent developed by the Tencent PC Manager team, has opened its international beta. The product is designed to let non-technical users deploy and interact with AI agents through messaging apps such as WhatsApp and Telegram without command-line setup or API configuration. The company said the international version was built in five days, with 99% of its code generated by QClaw itself.

Built on the OpenClaw framework, QClaw runs locally on users’ devices and supports long-term memory, model integration through API keys, and a range of agent templates for everyday tasks. Tencent said the Chinese version launched in March and drew more than 1 million users in its first 10 days. The international beta is now open to an initial wave of users, with 20,000 spots available. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: Amap debuts quadruped robot Tutu at Beijing humanoid half-marathon
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Amap, Alibaba’s mapping platform, debuted its quadruped robot Tutu at the 2026 Beijing E-Town Humanoid Robot Half-Marathon. The company said Tutu is designed to guide visually impaired users through complex open environments without preset routes or remote control, and demonstrated the robot navigating through crowds and obstacles at the event. Amap said Tutu is powered […]
item.content:encoded:
Amap, Alibaba’s mapping platform, debuted its quadruped robot Tutu at the 2026 Beijing E-Town Humanoid Robot Half-Marathon. The company said Tutu is designed to guide visually impaired users through complex open environments without preset routes or remote control, and demonstrated the robot navigating through crowds and obstacles at the event.

Amap said Tutu is powered by its in-house ABot technology framework, which combines spatial data, embodied AI models, and agent-based execution. The company has also open-sourced its ABot-M0 model as part of a broader push into robotics. The launch adds to Amap’s wider accessibility efforts, after it introduced wheelchair navigation in 2022 and visually impaired navigation in 2024. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: Huawei launches HarmonyOS AI smart glasses with camera and translation
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Huawei today launched its first HarmonyOS-powered AI smart glasses, which come with a 12MP camera and support quick image capture in 0.7 seconds. They also offer AI-assisted framing and first-person video recording. The device includes Huawei’s Xiaoyi AI assistant that provides real-time video interaction and simultaneous translation in 42 languages. The frame uses aerospace-grade titanium […]
item.content:encoded: Huawei today launched its first HarmonyOS-powered AI smart glasses, which come with a 12MP camera and support quick image capture in 0.7 seconds. They also offer AI-assisted framing and first-person video recording. The device includes Huawei’s Xiaoyi AI assistant that provides real-time video interaction and simultaneous translation in 42 languages. The frame uses aerospace-grade titanium alloy and weighs about 30 to 31 grams. The glasses have three built-in lithium batteries, offering up to eight hours of use and supporting magnetic fast charging. The product starts at 2,499 yuan ($345). [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: ByteDance 2025 profit drops over 70% amid surging AI investment
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: According to sources, ByteDance’s net profit in 2025 declined by more than 70% year-on-year, with its net profit margin also seeing a significant drop. This was attributed to the company’s substantial increase in investment in AI businesses during the third and fourth quarters of last year. At the same time, ByteDance’s overseas revenue in 2025 […]
item.content:encoded:
According to sources, ByteDance’s net profit in 2025 declined by more than 70% year-on-year, with its net profit margin also seeing a significant drop. This was attributed to the company’s substantial increase in investment in AI businesses during the third and fourth quarters of last year. At the same time, ByteDance’s overseas revenue in 2025 grew by nearly 50%, far outpacing the roughly 20% growth in its domestic business.

The share of overseas revenue in the company’s revenue rose from 25% in 2024 to over 30%. TikTok Shop’s gross merchandise value (GMV) in 2025 increased by nearly 70% year-on-year, becoming the main driver behind the rising share of ByteDance’s overseas revenue. Currently, ByteDance did not comment on the above figures. [Jiemian, in Chinese]

item.title: Xiaomi miclaw among first approved in China CAICT Claw evaluation
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Xiaomi said its Xiaomi miclaw mobile intelligent agent has become one of the first systems to pass the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology (CAICT) Claw smartphone intelligent assistant evaluation, marking a regulatory approval for advanced on-device AI systems. The Chinese smartphone maker said Xiaomi miclaw is powered by its in-house Xiaomi MiMo large […]
item.content:encoded:
Xiaomi said its Xiaomi miclaw mobile intelligent agent has become one of the first systems to pass the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology (CAICT) Claw smartphone intelligent assistant evaluation, marking a regulatory approval for advanced on-device AI systems. The Chinese smartphone maker said Xiaomi miclaw is powered by its in-house Xiaomi MiMo large language model and a device-side priority architecture, enabling what it described as four core capabilities: ecosystem-wide integration, deep memory understanding, cross-domain connectivity, and continuous self-improvement.

According to Xiaomi, the system can link smartphones, PCs, in-car platforms and AIoT devices, and is capable of autonomously executing complex user instructions across different environments. The company said Xiaomi miclaw is currently being rolled out in a limited testing programme for technology enthusiasts and advanced users. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: Honor’s humanoid robot breaks human half marathon record in 50min 26sec
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: On Sunday, at the Beijing Yizhuang Humanoid Robot Half Marathon, Honor’s “Flash” robot won the championship with a time of 50 minutes and 26 seconds, setting a new record and surpassing the human half marathon world record of 56 minutes and 42 seconds. The course measured 21.0975 kilometers and featured a variety of complex terrains. […]
item.content:encoded: On Sunday, at the Beijing Yizhuang Humanoid Robot Half Marathon, Honor’s “Flash” robot won the championship with a time of 50 minutes and 26 seconds, setting a new record and surpassing the human half marathon world record of 56 minutes and 42 seconds. The course measured 21.0975 kilometers and featured a variety of complex terrains. The “Flash” robot stands 169cm tall and adopts a sleek, mech-inspired design. It is equipped with autonomous perception and navigation capabilities, along with a self-developed high-dynamic motion system. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: China authority says daily AI token usage exceeds 140 trillion in March, up over 40% vs end-2025
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: China’s daily average token usage exceeded 140 trillion in March, up more than 40% from the end of 2025, a senior statistics official said on Thursday, underscoring rapid progress in the commercialisation and large-scale deployment of AI. Mao Shengyong, deputy head of the National Bureau of Statistics, told a State Council Information Office briefing that […]
item.content:encoded:
China’s daily average token usage exceeded 140 trillion in March, up more than 40% from the end of 2025, a senior statistics official said on Thursday, underscoring rapid progress in the commercialisation and large-scale deployment of AI. Mao Shengyong, deputy head of the National Bureau of Statistics, told a State Council Information Office briefing that China has made phased breakthroughs in scaling AI applications across industries.

The expansion of AI is boosting growth in related sectors. In the first quarter, value-added output of digital product manufacturing above designated size rose 11.2% from a year earlier, Mao said. Output in industries directly tied to AI production and use grew sharply, with electronic special materials manufacturing up 32.5% and integrated circuit manufacturing rising 49.4%, he added. [China Star Market, in Chinese]

item.title: Tesla considers humanoid robot production at Shanghai Gigafactory
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Tesla is considering producing humanoid robots at its Shanghai Gigafactory. The plant has been manufacturing electric vehicles since 2019 and began producing large-scale energy storage batteries in 2025. The new plan would expand its manufacturing scope to include humanoid robots, aiming to sustain the company’s growth momentum in the Chinese market. Wang Hao, President of […]
item.content:encoded:
Tesla is considering producing humanoid robots at its Shanghai Gigafactory. The plant has been manufacturing electric vehicles since 2019 and began producing large-scale energy storage batteries in 2025. The new plan would expand its manufacturing scope to include humanoid robots, aiming to sustain the company’s growth momentum in the Chinese market.

Wang Hao, President of Tesla China, said that as the era of robotics arrives, the Shanghai Gigafactory, like Tesla’s other factories, will play a crucial role as a major production base and contribute to products such as robots. Tesla has not disclosed details regarding the timeline or production scale of the potential project. [Jiemian, in Chinese]
